phrases.intent_en: Ask how to get to Place Ville-Marie from the metro
phrases.text_local: Comment est-ce qu'on rejoint Place Ville-Marie en passant par le souterrain ?
phrases.pronunciation: ko-MAH ess-KON ruh-ZHWAN plass veel-mah-REE ahn pah-SAHN par luh soo-teh-RAN
phrases.cultural_note: RÉSO is the world's largest underground pedestrian network — 33km of tunnels connecting metro stations, shopping, and offices. Essential in winter.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the underground connects to the train station
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que le souterrain est relié à la gare Centrale ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess-KUH luh soo-teh-RAN ay ruh-lee-YAY ah lah GAR sahn-TRAHL
phrases.cultural_note: Gare Centrale (Central Station) is fully connected to RÉSO, allowing travellers to arrive by train and reach most downtown hotels without going outside.

responses.english: Yes, follow the signs to Central Station.
responses.phonetic: wee swee-VAY lay pah-NOH GAR sahn-TRAHL
responses.french: Oui, suivez les panneaux Gare Centrale.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the nearest food court is underground
phrases.text_local: Il y a une aire de restauration souterraine par ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel ee ah OON air duh res-toh-rah-SYON soo-teh-REN par ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: RÉSO has numerous food courts, especially under major complexes like Les Promenades Cathédrale and Complexe Les Ailes.

responses.english: There is one on the lower level, that way.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ahn ah uhn oh nee-VOH ahn-fay-RYUR par LAH
responses.french: Il y en a un au niveau inférieur, par là.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for directions to the Eaton Centre from underground
phrases.text_local: Pour aller au Centre Eaton par le souterrain, c'est par où ?
phrases.pronunciation: poor ah-LAY oh SAHN-truh ee-TON par luh soo-teh-RAN say par OO
phrases.cultural_note: Les Galeries du Centre (formerly Eaton Centre) is one of the main underground shopping hubs. Signs in RÉSO are in French only.

phrases.intent_en: Say you are lost in the tunnels
phrases.text_local: Je me suis perdu dans les tunnels. Pouvez-vous m'aider ?
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh muh swee pair-DOO dahn lay too-NEL. poo-VAY voo may-DAY
phrases.cultural_note: Getting turned around in RÉSO is very common even for locals. Don't hesitate to ask passersby — Montréalers are famously helpful.

responses.english: Look for a map on the walls. The metro is that way.
responses.phonetic: shair-SHAY uhn PLAHN soor lay MOOR luh may-TROH eh par LAH
responses.french: Cherchez un plan sur les murs. Le métro est par là.
